en.summary: The Prophet Muhammad (SAWW), through a chain going back via the Imams, identifies four categories of people whose supplications are guaranteed acceptance by God and ascend directly to the Divine Throne. This hadith was narrated in the assembly of al-Saduq in Safar 368 AH, reflecting the importance early Shia scholars placed on the spiritual efficacy of du'a in specific states of devotion and vulnerability.

key_terms.دعوة: Supplication (du'a) — a prayer or invocation to God
key_terms.العرش: The Throne (al-'Arsh) — the divine Throne of God, representing the highest realm
key_terms.المعتمر: One performing Umrah — the lesser pilgrimage to Mecca
key_terms.الصائم: The fasting person — one observing sawm (fasting)

en.seo_question: Whose supplications are never rejected according to Prophet Muhammad?

ur.summary: نبی کریم (صلی اللہ علیہ وآلہ) نے چار لوگوں کی دعاؤں کی قبولیت کی ضمانت دی ہے جو عرش تک پہنچتی ہیں: والدین، مظلوم، عمرہ کرنے والے اور روزہ دار۔

key_terms.العرش: عرش — اللہ کا تخت، اعلیٰ مقام

ur.seo_question: نبی کریم کے مطابق کن لوگوں کی دعا رد نہیں ہوتی؟

tr.summary: Hz. Peygamber (s.a.v.s.) dört grubun duasının asla reddedilmeyeceğini ve doğrudan Arş'a ulaşacağını bildirmiştir: ebeveyn, mazlum, umreci ve oruçlu.
tr.seo_question: Hz. Peygamber'e göre kimlerin duası reddedilmez?

fa.summary: پیامبر اکرم (صلی الله علیه وآله) چهار گروه را معرفی فرمودند که دعایشان هرگز رد نمی‌شود و مستقیماً به عرش الهی می‌رسد: والدین، مظلوم، حج‌گزار عمره و روزه‌دار.

key_terms.المعتمر: معتمر — کسی که عمره به جا می‌آورد

fa.seo_question: طبق فرموده پیامبر دعای چه کسانی رد نمی‌شود؟

id.summary: Nabi Muhammad (SAW) menyebutkan empat golongan yang doanya dijamin dikabulkan dan naik langsung ke Arsy: orang tua, orang yang dizalimi, orang yang berumrah, dan orang yang berpuasa.
id.seo_question: Siapa saja yang doanya tidak pernah ditolak menurut Nabi Muhammad?

bn.summary: নবী মুহাম্মাদ (সা.) চার শ্রেণীর মানুষের দু'আর নিশ্চিত কবুলের কথা বলেছেন যা সরাসরি আরশে পৌঁছায়: পিতামাতা, মজলুম, উমরাকারী ও রোজাদার।
bn.seo_question: নবী মুহাম্মাদের মতে কাদের দু'আ কখনো প্রত্যাখ্যান হয় না?

es.summary: El Profeta Muhammad (PBSCE) identificó cuatro personas cuyas súplicas están garantizadas de ser aceptadas y ascienden directamente al Trono Divino: el padre, el oprimido, el peregrino y el ayunante.
es.seo_question: ¿Según el Profeta Muhammad, cuáles son las súplicas que nunca son rechazadas?

fr.summary: Le Prophète Muhammad (PSLF) a identifié quatre catégories de personnes dont les invocations sont assurément acceptées et montent directement au Trône Divin : le parent, l'opprimé, le pèlerin d'Oumra et le jeûneur.
fr.seo_question: Selon le Prophète Muhammad, quelles invocations ne sont jamais rejetées ?

de.summary: Der Prophet Muhammad (SAWS) nannte vier Gruppen von Menschen, deren Bittgebete garantiert erhört werden und direkt zum göttlichen Thron aufsteigen: Eltern, Unterdrückte, Umra-Pilger und Fastende.
de.seo_question: Wessen Bittgebete werden laut Prophet Muhammad nie abgewiesen?

ru.summary: Пророк Мухаммад (ДБАР) назвал четыре категории людей, чьи мольбы гарантированно принимаются и поднимаются прямо к Божественному Трону: родители, угнетённые, паломники умры и постящиеся.
ru.seo_question: Чьи мольбы никогда не отвергаются согласно Пророку Мухаммаду?

zh.summary: 先知穆罕默德（愿主赐福他和他的家人）指出四类人的祈祷必定被接受并直达神圣宝座：父母、受压迫者、副朝者和斋戒者。

key_terms.العرش: 宝座（阿尔什）——神圣的宝座

zh.seo_question: 根据先知穆罕默德，谁的祈祷永远不会被拒绝？

isnad_matn.isnad_ar: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ الْوَلِيدِ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارُ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ أَبِي الْخَطَّابِ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ النُّعْمَانِ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ طَلْحَةَ النَّهْدِيِّ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ آبَائِهِ عَلَيْهِمُ السَّلَامُ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ
isnad_matn.matn_ar: أَرْبَعَةٌ لَا تُرَدُّ لَهُمْ دَعْوَةٌ وَتُفْتَحُ لَهَا أَبْوَابُ السَّمَاءِ وَتَصِيرُ إِلَى الْعَرْشِ دُعَاءُ الْوَالِدِ لِوَلَدِهِ وَالْمَظْلُومُ عَلَى مَنْ ظَلَمَهُ وَالْمُعْتَمِرُ حَتَّى يَرْجِعَ وَالصَّائِمُ حَتَّى يُفْطِرَ

narrators.name_ar: مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ الْوَلِيدِ
narrators.name_en: Muhammad ibn al-Hasan ibn Ahmad ibn al-Walid
narrators.known_identity: Muhammad ibn al-Hasan ibn Ahmad ibn al-Walid al-Qummi (d. 343 AH), prominent Shia hadith scholar and teacher of al-Saduq
Muhammad ibn al-Hasan al-Saffar al-Qummi (d. 290 AH), author of Basa'ir al-Darajat

narrators.name_ar: مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ أَبِي الْخَطَّابِ
narrators.name_en: Muhammad ibn al-Husayn ibn Abi al-Khattab
narrators.known_identity: Muhammad ibn al-Husayn ibn Abi al-Khattab al-Zayyat (d. 262 AH), major Shia hadith transmitter
Ali ibn al-Nu'man al-A'lam al-Nakha'i, companion of Imam al-Rida (AS)
Abdullah ibn Talha al-Nahdi, narrator from the companions of Imam al-Sadiq (AS)
Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq (AS), the sixth Imam (d. 148 AH)

narrators.ambiguity_note: Refers to Imam Muhammad al-Baqir (AS), the fifth Imam (d. 114 AH), father of Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq
narrators.known_identity: Imam Muhammad al-Baqir (AS), the fifth Imam

narrators.ambiguity_note: Refers to the chain of Imams back to Imam Ali (AS) and the Prophet Muhammad (SAWW)
narrators.known_identity: The preceding Imams: Imam Ali Zayn al-Abidin, Imam al-Husayn, and Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (AS)
Prophet Muhammad (SAWW), the final Prophet of Islam

key_phrases.phrase_en: May God bless him and his family
May God be pleased with him

similar_content_hints.description: Similar hadiths enumerate categories of people whose du'a is accepted, such as the traveler, the just ruler, and the du'a of a Muslim for his brother in his absence.
Hadiths about the virtues of fasting emphasize that the fasting person has a supplication that is answered at the time of breaking the fast.
Narrations about the du'a of the oppressed being answered without barrier between it and God, found across multiple hadith collections.
